review_scope.summary: Reviewed only the repaired stop-path implementation in `new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp`, the listed checkpoint-4 board logs, and the approved change artifacts as reference material.
focus: diagnostics-only stop completion keys off effective command zero rather than suppressed requested PWM
real-motor STOPPING converges applied command to zero under lifecycle ownership
wrapper-owned frame-budget stop reaches DISARMED and process exit on diagnostics-only and motor-enabled runs
no obvious regression of accepted Phase B SPINUP/RUNNING/STOPPING semantics within the declared repair surface

notes: The repaired stop-path logic was verified in `new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp` at the STOPPING ramp-down and stop-completion re-evaluation path.
Diagnostics-only rerun evidence shows `motion.stop.complete` followed by `main.exit.ready` and clean process exit.
Motor-enabled rerun v2 evidence shows `DISARMED` transition, actuator disarm, `main.exit.ready`, and clean process exit.
The earlier motor-enabled fix log was reviewed as historical checkpoint evidence; the pass decision relies on the repaired code plus the successful rerun evidence.

skipped_paths: openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/verification/checkpoint-4/* (unchanged artifacts outside the caller-declared rerun scope)
